demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged-care (nursing home) facility; uses a walking frame and needs help with daily activities

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: This hard lump on my hand has been growing for weeks and throbbing, and today it just burst open and won't stop bleeding. (Reported with the facility registered nurse present, who is holding blood-soaked gauze to the back of the hand.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A hard lump on the back of the hand suddenly split open a couple of hours ago after a thick scab fell off, and it has been bleeding heavily ever since — the nursing staff pressed on it but could not get it to stop, and everyone is worried about the amount of blood.

symptom_narrative.onset: a flat rough scaly patch had been on the back of the left hand for a long time, then over the last six weeks it turned into a hard lump that grew quickly
symptom_narrative.duration: rapidly growing lump over about six weeks; became tender and painful over the last day; has been bleeding heavily for the last two hours after a thick crust came off on its own
symptom_narrative.character: a hard, firm, raised lump that had built up a thick hard crust or 'horn' on top; the crust came away today leaving a raw sore in the middle that is now oozing dark red blood
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: frightening and messy — it has soaked through blood-stained gauze and the staff cannot get it to stop; the lump throbs and is now sore to touch
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: on the back (top) of the left hand
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the lump grew steadily over six weeks; the pain came on over the last day; the heavy bleeding started about two hours ago when the crust detached
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: pressing hard on it with gauze slows the bleeding a little but it starts again
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: the crust coming off, and any knock or movement of the hand restarts the bleeding
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no fever, no spreading redness up the arm; the lump itself was not painful until the last day
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no heavy bleeding like this before, though the lump had grown and crusted over the past weeks
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: frail nursing-home resident who uses a walking frame; anxious and keeping the hand elevated and wrapped

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: generally frail and needs help with daily activities

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a blood-thinning tablet called apixaban (Eliquis), one 5 mg tablet twice a day for the irregular heartbeat

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a residential aged-care facility with nursing staff on site; a registered nurse is present and helping during this contact; a lifetime of sun exposure on the hands

objective_data_offered.value: a firm, raised, roughly 2 to 3 cm reddened lump on the back of the left hand; a thick hard crusty scale has come off the top, leaving a sunken raw sore in the centre that is slowly and steadily oozing dark red blood
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: described from a video/photo view and the nurse's description; not a clinician skin examination or dermoscopy, and the depth of the lump cannot be judged remotely

objective_data_offered.value: the blood is a slow, steady dark-red ooze rather than a fast spurt; it has soaked through several layers of gauze but is not squirting
frail but alert, mildly anxious, sitting up with the left hand elevated and wrapped in blood-stained gauze

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: measured and relayed by the facility nurse on a ward cuff during this contact; may be elevated by anxiety and pain
relayed by the facility nurse; patient has known atrial fibrillation
facility fingertip oximeter reading relayed by the nurse
